rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical metal-free 1,2-syn-cyano-hydroxylation of alkenes via a telescoped 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition with a sulfonyl-nitrile oxide followed by cathodic SET-induced radical ring-opening (sew & cut)
electrochemical_role: Cathodic SET reduction of the C=N (oxime) moiety of the 3-sulfonyl-isoxazoline cycloadduct generates a radical anion that expels arylsulfinate to form an imidoyl radical, which undergoes β-scission; anode performs oxidation of Et3N (or 1,4-cyclohexadiene) as sacrificial reductant to close the circuit

key_intermediates.name: radical anion IX (cathodic SET adduct of cycloadduct)
α-oxy radical VIII from radical Brook rearrangement (with C5-silyl substrate)

electron_flow: In situ generated sulfonyl-nitrile oxide undergoes metal-free 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition with the alkene to give an isoxazoline cycloadduct; cathodic single-electron reduction of its C=N bond yields a radical anion that ejects arylsulfinate to form an imidoyl radical, which fragments by radical β-scission of the N–O bond to release a nitrile and an oxygen-centered radical, the latter being further reduced at the cathode and protonated to deliver the 1,2-syn-cyano-hydroxylation product.
rate_determining_step: Radical β-scission of the imidoyl radical X (isoxazoline ring-opening) following SET reduction and sulfinate loss

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropene-derived cycloadduct 18 (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis afforded β-hydroxy aldehyde 19 (70%) and over-reduced alcohol 20 (28%); no β-hydroxy-imine or β-hydroxy-amine (excluding path A) and no tertiary-radical product 21/22 from cyclopropane opening (excluding path C), consistent with path B: SET to C=N, sulfinate loss, then fast isoxazoline β-scission outcompeting cyclopropane opening

probe_reagents.name: 3-bromo-isoxazoline 12
probe_reagents.observation: Gave product 9 in 33% with 66% recovered SM, showing a good anionic leaving group at C3 is required

probe_reagents.name: 3-ester isoxazoline 13 and 3-alkyl isoxazoline 14
probe_reagents.observation: No fragmentation product formed; confirms need for an expellable anionic LG (ArSO2-, Br) at C3

probe_reagents.name: C3-carboxylate 15 (anodic decarboxylation under alternating polarity)
probe_reagents.observation: Delivered 9 in 9% NMR yield, showing pendant redox handle can also trigger ring-opening

probe_reagents.name: NHPI ester 16 (cathodic activation)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave syn-hydroxy nitrile 9 in complex mixture, supporting analogous radical fragmentation

probe_reagents.name: C5-trimethylsilyl cycloadduct 23 (Brook probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Furnished silyl ether 24 (64%) via radical Brook rearrangement of oxygen-centered radical VII to α-oxy radical VIII, confirming oxygen-centered radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of cycloadduct 8 and analogs 12–16
probe_reagents.observation: Two reduction peaks at Epc = -1.01 V (oxime C=N) and -2.10 V (aryl sulfone); first reduction at oxime triggers fragmentation

probe_reagents.name: 1,4-cyclohexadiene (sacrificial reductant at anode)
probe_reagents.observation: Serves as competent anodic sacrificial reductant, easing purification by avoiding nonvolatile byproducts

notes: Three pathways were considered for cycloadduct fragmentation: path A (cathodic N–O cleavage to distonic radical anion III) was ruled out by absence of β-hydroxy-imine/amine; path C (cyclopropane ring-opening dominant) was ruled out by absence of stabilized tertiary-radical products 21/22; favored path B involves SET to C=N then sulfinate loss and β-scission. Minor side pathway: with C5-silyl substrates a radical Brook rearrangement diverts the oxygen-centered radical to an α-oxy radical, giving silyl ether instead of the cyano-hydroxylation product. Furoxan 11 (nitrile oxide dimer) is a stoichiometric byproduct consumed during the first ~1 F/mol of electrolysis.
